ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
acetyl: noun. The univalent radical CH 3 CO- derived from acetic acid.
act: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Achterhooks.
active: adjective. Having the power or quality of acting; causing change; communicating action or motion; acting;, opposed to passive, that
acyl: noun. Any of class of organic radicals, RCO-, formed by the removal of a hydroxyl group from a carboxylic acid.
ail: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimele.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
ale: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Aleut.
alec: noun. An anchovy or herring, especially pickled or dried.
alit: verb. simple past and past participle of alight
alive: adjective. Having life; living; not dead.
alt: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Altai.
ate: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Atemble.
atelic: adjective. Presenting an action or event’s significance as being the process or experience instead of the end accomplishment or goal.
ave: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avestan.
aye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ayere.
cat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Catalan.
cate: noun. A delicacy or item of food.
cave: noun. A large, naturally-occurring cavity formed underground or in the face of a cliff or a hillside.
cavil: verb. To criticise for petty or frivolous reasons.
cavity: noun. A hole or hollow depression in a solid object.
cavy: noun. Synonym of domestic guinea pig.
clave: verb. simple past and past participle of cleave
clay: noun. A mineral substance made up of small crystals of silica and alumina, that is ductile when moist; the material of pre-fired ceramics.
cleat: noun. A strip of wood or iron fastened on transversely to something in order to give strength, prevent warping, hold position, etc.
evict: verb. To expel (one or more people) from their property; to force (one or more people) to move out.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
icy: adjective. Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.
ivy: noun. Any of several woody, climbing, or trailing evergreen plants of the genus Hedera.
lac: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lacandon.
lace: noun. A light fabric containing patterns of holes, usually built up from a single thread.
lacy: adjective. Made of lace or decorated with it.
laic: noun. A layperson as opposed to a cleric, i.e. not a member of the clergy.
lat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Latin.
late: adjective. Near the end of a period of time.
lav: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Latvian.
lave: verb. To bathe or wash (someone or something).
lay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lama Bai.
lea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lega-Shabunda.
let: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lesing-Gelimi.
lev: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lamma.
levity: noun. A lightness of manner or speech, frivolity; flippancy; a lack of appropriate seriousness; an inclination to make a joke of serious
levy: verb. To impose (a tax or fine) to collect monies due, or to confiscate property.
ley: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemolang.
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
live: verb. To be alive; to have life.
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
lytic: adjective. Of, relating to, or causing lysis.
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
tael: noun. Any of several units of measure used in China and elsewhere in eastern Asia, approximately 40 grams, and metricated in Mainland China
tail: noun. The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to their posterior and near the anus or cloaca.
talc: noun. Originally a large range of transparent or glistening foliated minerals.
tale: noun. A rehearsal of what has occurred; narrative; discourse; statement; history; story.
tav: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tatuyo.
tea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Temiar.
teal: noun. Any of various small freshwater ducks of the genus Anas that are brightly coloured and have short necks.
tel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Telugu.
tela: noun. A thin, web -like structure or membrane.
telic: adjective. Tending or directed towards a goal or specific end.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tical: noun. An old Thai measurement of weight, the baht, of about 15 grams.
til: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tillamook.
tile: noun. A regularly-shaped slab of clay or other material, affixed to cover or decorate a surface, as in a roof -tile, glazed tile, stove
tye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kyenga.
vac: noun. Clipping of vacation (“extended period of time away from work or school”).
valet: noun. A man's personal male attendant, responsible for his clothes and appearance.
vat: noun. A large tub, such as is used for making wine or for tanning.
vatic: adjective. Pertaining to a prophet; prophetic, oracular.
veal: noun. The flesh of a calf (i.e. a young bovine) used for food.
veil: noun. Something hung up or spread out to hide or protect the face, or hide an object from view; usually of gauze, crepe, or similar
vet: noun. A veterinarian or veterinary surgeon.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vial: noun. A glass or plastic vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemicals.
vice: noun. Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
vie: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Vietnamese.
vilayet: noun. A province, particularly the first - level administrative divisions of the Ottoman Empire.
vita: noun. A hagiography; a biography of a saint.
vital: adjective. Relating to or characteristic of life.
yea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ravula.
yet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yetfa.
yeti: noun. An unidentified humanoid animal said to live in the Himalayas.
